records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: Some people say this saying is of the Prophet (s.a.w.), but what confirms that it is the saying of Amīr al-mu’minīn (a.s.), is the statement related by Tha‘lab from Ibn al-A‘rābī namely that (Caliph) al-Ma’mūn said: “If ‘Alī had not said ‘ukhbur taqlihi’ (Try a man and you will hate him), I would have said, ‘aqlihi takhbur’ (Hate a man in order to try him).”
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضي: ومن الناس من يروي هذا لرسول الله (صلى الله عليه وآله)، ومما يُقوّي أنه من كلام أميرالمؤمنين (عليه السلام) ما حكاه ثعلب قال: حدّثنا ابن الأعرابي قال: قال المأمون: لو لا أنّ علياً (عليه السلام) قال: «اخبُر تَقْلِهِ» لقلت أنا: اقْلِهِ تَخْبُرْ.

records.chapter: Leadership suitability based on honorable lineage
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 436. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The most appropriate person for an honourable position is he who descends from the people of honour.
records.arabicText: ٤٣٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أوْلَى النَّاسِ بِالْكَرَمِ مَنْ عَرَقتْ فيه الْكِرَامُ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 437. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, was asked: Which of the two is better; justice or generosity? Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, replied:
records.arabicText: ٤٣٧. وَسُئِلَ (علیه السلام) أَیُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ: الْعَدْلُ أَوِ الْجُودُ؟ فَقَالَ (علیه السلام):

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Justice puts things in their places while generosity takes them out from their directions; justice is the general caretaker while generosity is a particular benefit. Consequently, justice is superior and more distinguished of the two.
records.arabicText: الْعَدْلُ يَضَعُ الْأُمُورَ مَوَاضِعَهَا، والْجُودُ يُخْرِجُهَا عَنْ جِهَتِهَا، وَالْعَدْلُ سَائِسٌ عَامٌّ، وَالْجُودُ عَارضٌ خَاصٌّ، فَالْعَدْلُ أَشْرَفُهُمَا وَأَفْضَلُهُمَا.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 439. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The whole of asceticism is confined between two expressions of the Qur’ān. Allāh, the Glorified says : “Lest distress you yourselves for what escapes you, and be overjoyous for what He has granted you” (Qur’ān, 57:23). Whoever does not grieve over what he misses and does not revel over what comes to him acquires asceticism from both its sides.
records.arabicText: ۴۳۹. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الزُّهْدُ كُلُّهُ بَيْنَ كَلِمَتَينِ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ: قَالَ اللهُ سُبْحَانَهُ : ﴿لِّكَيْلَا تَأْسَوْا عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا تَفْرَحُوا بِمَا آتَاكُمْ﴾، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَأْسَ عَلَى الْمَاضِي، وَلَمْ يَفْرَحْ بِالْآتِي، فَقَدْ أَخَذَ الزُّهْدَ بِطَرَفَيْهِ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 440. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: What a breaker is sleep for resolutions of the day!
records.arabicText: ٤٤٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا أَنْقَضَ النَّوْمَ لِعَزَائِمِ الْيَوْمِ.

records.chapter: Best town is where one thrives
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 442. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: No town has greater right on you than the other. The best town for you is that which bears you.
records.arabicText: ٤٤٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَيْسَ بَلَدٌ بأَحَقَّ بِكَ مِنْ بَلَدٍ، خَيْرُ الْبِلاَدِ مَا حَمَلَكَ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 443. When the news of the death of (Mālik) al-Ashtar (may Allāh have mercy on him), reached Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, he said:
records.arabicText: ٤٤٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) وَقَدْ جَاءَهُ نَعْيُ الْأَشْتَرِ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ:

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Mālik, what a man Mālik was! By Allāh, if he had been a mountain he would have been a big one (find), and if he had been a stone he would have been hard; no horseman could have reached it and no bird could have flown over it.
records.arabicText: مَالكٌ وَمَا مَالِكٌ! وَاللهِ لَوْ كَانَ جَبَلاً لَكَانَ فِنْداً، وَلَوْ كَانَ حَجَراً لَكَانَ صَلْداً، لَا يَرْتَقِيهِ الْحَافِرُ، وَلَا يُوفِي عَلَيْهِ الطَّائِرُ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: “find” means a lonely mountain (rising in height above the range).
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضي: الفند: المنفرد من الجبال.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 445. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If a man possesses a revealing quality wait and see his other qualities. [1]
records.arabicText: ٤٤٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إذَا كَانَ في رَجُلٍ خَلَّةٌ رَائِعَةٌ فَانْتَظِرُوا أَخَوَاتِهَا.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 446. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said to Ghālib ibn Ṣa‘ṣa‘ah, the father of al-Farazdaq (the famous Arabic poet) during a conversation between them: What about the large number of your camels?
records.arabicText: ٤٤٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِغَالِبِ بْنِ صَعْصَعَةَ أَبِی الْفَرَزْدَقِ، فِی كَلَامٍ دَارَ بَيْنَهُمَا: مَا فَعَلَتْ إِبِلُكَ الْكثِيرَةُ؟

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: The man replied: They have been swept away by (discharging of) obligations, O’ Amīr al-mu’minīn!
records.arabicText: قال: ذَعْذَعَتْها الحُقُوقُ يا أميرالمؤمنين.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Whereupon Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: That is the most praiseworthy way of (losing) them.
records.arabicText: فَقَالَ (عليه السلام) : ذَاكَ أَحْمَدُ سُبُلِهَا.

records.chapter: Enduring minor hardships prevents greater trials
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 448. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever regards small distresses as big, Allāh involves him in big ones.
records.arabicText: ٤٤٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ عَظَّمَ صِغَارَ الْمَصَائِبِ ابْتَلاَهُ اللهُ بِكِبَارِهَا.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 449. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Whoever maintains his own respect in view, his desires appear light to him.
records.arabicText: ٤٤٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْ كَرُمَتْ عَلَيْهِ نَفْسُهُ هَانَتْ عَلَيْهِ شَهْوَتُهُ.

records.chapter: Avoiding reciprocation leads to loss or humiliation
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 451. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Your turning away from him who inclines towards you is a loss of your share of advantage while your inclining towards him who turns away from you is humiliation for yourself.
records.arabicText: ٤٥١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): زُهْدُكَ فِي رَاغِبٍ فِيكَ نُقْصَانُ حَظٍّ، وَرَغْبَتُكَ فِي زَاهِدٍ فِيكَ ذُلُّ نَفْسٍ.

records.chapter: Riches and destitution follow divine judgment
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 452. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Riches and destitution will follow presentation before Allāh.
records.arabicText: ۴۵۲. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْغِنَى وَالْفَقْرُ بَعْدَ الْعَرْضِ عَلَى اللهِ.

records.chapter: Human mortality and the folly of vanity
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 454. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: What has a man to do with vanity. His origin is semen and his end is a carcass while he cannot feed himself nor ward off death. [1]
records.arabicText: ٤٥٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا لِابْنِ آدَمَ وَالْفَخْرِ: أَوَّلُهُ نُطْفَةٌ، وَآخِرُهُ جِيفَةٌ، وَلَا يَرْزُقُ نَفْسَهُ، وَلَا يَدفَعُ حَتْفَهُ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 456. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Is there no free man who can leave this chewed morsel (of the world) to those who like it? Certainly, the only price for yourselves is Paradise. Therefore, do not sell yourself except for Paradise.
records.arabicText: ٤٥٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أَلَا حُرٌّ يَدَعُ هذِهِ اللُّمَاظَةَ لِأَهْلِهَا؟ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ ثَمَنٌ إِلَّا الْجَنَّةَ، فَلاَ تَبِيعُوهَا إِلَّا بِهَا.

records.chapter: Insatiable desires for knowledge and worldly pursuits
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 457. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Two greedy persons never get satiated, the seeker of knowledge and the seeker of this world.
records.arabicText: ٤٥٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَنْهُومَانِ لَا يَشْبَعَانِ: طَالِبُ عِلْمٍ، وَطَالِبُ دُنْيَا.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 459. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Destiny holds sway over (our) predetermination till effort itself brings about ruin.
records.arabicText: ٤٥٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): يَغْلِبُ الْمِقْدَارُعَلَى التَّقْديرِ حَتَّى تَكُونَ الْآفَةُ فِي التَّدْبِيرِ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: Something of this meaning has already appeared earlier though in words different from these.
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضي: وقد مضى هذا المعنى فيما تقدم برواية تخالف هذه الألفاظ.

records.chapter: Forbearance and endurance as virtues of courage
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 460. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Forbearance and endurance are twins and they are the product of high courage.
records.arabicText: ٤٦٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْحِلْمُ وَالْأَنَاةُ تَوْأَمَانِ يُنْتِجُهُمَا عُلُوُّ الْهِمَّةِ.

records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 462. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Many a man gets into mischief because of being spoken well about.
records.arabicText: ٤٦٢. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): رُبَّ مَفْتُونٍ بِحُسْنِ الْقَوْلِ فِيهِ.
